item.title: Enhance simulating accuracy with lua
item.content:encoded:
May 2, 2026

Updated May 2, 2026

Enhance simulating accuracy with lua



Now you can enhance the accuracy of your car simulation by using Lua scripts On the sim page of the app



Then edit or copy paste your script in the editor, and save it. The script will be executed every time a command is sent to the simulator, allowing you to customize the responses based on the commands received. The ELM327 layer is already handled by the app, so you can focus on simulating the vehicle behavior and responses to OBD-II/UDS/custom commands.



As always you can check result of your sim in the log page and see the commands sent and responses received in real time.

Feedback for features and suggestions are always welcome, and if you have any questions or need help with the app, feel free to reach out to me. autodiag at netcourrier dot com or in github issues

item.title: autodiag
item.content:encoded:
April 5, 2026

Updated April 5, 2026

autodiag

Autodiag is another automotive diagnostic scantool focused on open-source development and protocol experimentation.

It targets developers and advanced users who need low-level access to vehicle diagnostic protocols rather than a consumer-oriented interface.

The project is available on GitHub: https://github.com/autodiag2/autodiag

item.title: Autodiag database 🚀
item.content:encoded:
April 5, 2026

Updated April 5, 2026

Autodiag database 🚀

Keep track of an up-to-date DTC list, with checking of dups, importing list editing it and more !

Data browser and editor

Query the database

To install check manager doc

item.title: Autodiag v41
item.content:encoded:
April 5, 2026

Updated April 5, 2026

Autodiag v41

Thanks to signals, an abstraction layer over the diagnostic data, anything built ontop of libautodiag has capabilities to send, generate and receive signal below is an example use case with sim generator gui.

Launched with: 

        ./output/bin/elm327sim -g gui
        ./output/bin/autodiag gui -n /dev/ttys001
This will allow to feed the lib with time series, improve the android app and maybe in the future support CAN signals with DBC format.

item.title: Simulating a car from your Android phone
item.content:encoded:
April 5, 2026

Updated April 5, 2026

Simulating a car from your Android phone

You can simulate a car directly from your Android phone using the ELM327SimAndroid application.

Download the application from GitHub releases and install it on your device.

Once installed, start the simulator. The application runs a virtual ELM327 interface on your phone, emulating a vehicle ECU and standard OBD-II responses.

You can then connect your preferred diagnostic tool to the simulator, exactly as if you were connected to a real car. For example, you can use AndrOBD , an Android OBD diagnostic application compatible with ELM327 devices.

This setup is useful for testing diagnostic applications, developing scantools, or experimenting with OBD-II protocols without requiring physical vehicle hardware.

Inspecting the data sent by the scantool to ELM:

Change the adapter emulation kind (Bluetooth, Bluetooth Low Energy, Wifi)
